title: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken

seo.title: Chicken Cacciatore Recipe (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) with this easy, flavorful recipe. Perfect for a hearty family meal!
lowCompetitionKeywords: Chicken Cacciatore with olives and bell peppers
seo.h1: Authentic Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)
h2Headings: What is Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora)?
Ingredients You'll Need for Hunter Style Chicken
Step-by-Step Instructions for Pollo alla Cacciatora
Pro Tips for the Best Chicken Cacciatore
Serving Suggestions for Your Italian Feast
seo.imageAlt: A delicious plate of Italian Chicken Cacciatore (Pollo alla Cacciatora) garnished with fresh herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, rustic flavors of this classic Italian Hunter Style Chicken, a perfect hearty meal.

description: Immerse yourself in the rustic charm of Pollo alla Cacciatora, a classic Italian Hunter Style Chicken dish that brings robust flavors to your table. Tender chicken pieces are braised in a rich tomato sauce with bell peppers, onions, mushrooms, and olives, creating a deeply satisfying and aromatic meal. This hearty recipe is perfect for a comforting family dinner, embodying the essence of traditional Italian home cooking.

ingredients.notes: like Pinot Grigio or Sauvignon Blanc

instructions.title: Brown the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels and season generously with salt and black pepper. Heat olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the chicken in batches, skin-side down first, until deeply golden brown on all sides, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove chicken from the pot and set aside.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Vegetables
instructions.text: Reduce heat to medium. Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened and translucent, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the sliced red and green bell peppers and mushrooms. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables start to soften.

instructions.title: Deglaze with Wine
instructions.text: Pour in the dry white wine and scrape the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon to loosen any browned bits. Let the wine simmer for 2-3 minutes, allowing most of the alcohol to evaporate.

instructions.title: Add Tomatoes and Herbs
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, fresh rosemary, fresh thyme, and bay leaf. Season with a pinch more salt and pepper. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer.

instructions.title: Braise the Chicken
instructions.text: Return the browned chicken pieces to the pot, nestling them into the sauce. Ensure the chicken is mostly submerged. Cover the pot, reduce heat to low, and let it simmer gently for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and tender.

instructions.title: Add Olives and Finish
instructions.text: Uncover the pot. Stir in the pitted Kalamata olives and continue to simmer for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf and herb sprigs before serving. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley. Pollo alla Cacciatora is excellent served immediately with crusty bread, polenta, or pasta.

tips: For extra depth of flavor, marinate the chicken in a little olive oil, garlic, and herbs for at least 30 minutes before browning.
Dont overcrowd the pan when browning the chicken; work in batches to ensure a good sear and even browning, which adds crucial flavor.
If you prefer a thicker sauce, remove the chicken once tender, then simmer the sauce uncovered for an additional 10-15 minutes until it reaches your desired consistency.
article: Pollo alla Cacciatora Hunter Style Chicken is a beloved Italian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
